clientConfigPage.title: Client Setup
clientConfigPage.subtitle: Write the reverse proxy or a third-party provider into AI client configs in one click (auto-backup, revertible).
clientConfigPage.empty: No profiles for this client yet
clientConfigPage.emptyHint: Click "Add Profile" to write a provider into its config
clientConfigPage.coexistHint: Multiple providers can be injected at once — account & third-party models coexist
clientConfigPage.switchHint: Only one provider is active at a time; switching overwrites
clientConfigPage.relayInjectionHint: When on, Codex connects only to the 号小管 proxy; account + third-party models are aggregated in /model (true coexistence). When off, providers switch individually.
clientConfigPage.routingHint: When on, this client's third-party providers are relayed via the 号小管 proxy (protocol conversion + account pool / IP). Providers whose protocol differs from the client require it. The API service auto-starts on enable if it's not running.
clientConfigPage.routingOffBlocked: The active provider's protocol differs from the client; keep Routing on to use it. Switch providers before turning it off.

needRelayDialog.title: Relay required
needRelayDialog.desc: "{{name}}" uses protocol {{proto}}, which differs from {{client}} ({{native}}); it must be converted & relayed via the 号小管 proxy. Turn on Routing and enable it?

clientConfigPage.accountProviderHint: Use your account quota via the local reverse proxy
clientConfigPage.emptyThirdParty: No third-party providers yet — click "Add provider"

form.previewHint: Enter a URL to preview the config to be written
form.previewRelayNote: On enable, the URL is rewritten to the local reverse proxy
form.mismatchHint: Upstream protocol differs from this client: turn on Routing in the list to convert & relay via the 号小管 proxy when enabling.
form.modelMapHint: Set the actual model per tier; display name only affects the /model menu; tick 1M to declare long context. Blank tiers are skipped
form.thirdPartyHint: Enter a third-party provider URL & key (direct). For the reverse proxy, use "Connect local proxy".
form.name: Name
form.fullUrlHintOff: Treated as a base address; /v1 is appended automatically (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8080 → …/v1). No trailing slash.
form.fullUrlHintOn: Used exactly as entered, no /v1 appended (custom gateway / already ends in /v1 / upstream without /v1). No trailing slash.
form.upstreamProtocolHint: If it differs from this client's protocol, traffic is converted & relayed via the local proxy
form.routingForcedHint: This upstream protocol differs from the client's; traffic must be converted & relayed via the local proxy.
form.routingHint: When on, third-party traffic is relayed via the proxy (account pool / IP). The API service auto-starts on enable if it's not running.
form.codexModelListHint: Generates the Codex model_catalog_json for the /model menu. Changes take effect after restarting Codex.

clientConfigPage.codexSwitchDesc: After switching to "{{name}}", older sessions may become invisible in Codex due to provider filtering. Enable the toggle below to migrate history sessions to the new provider so they remain visible.
clientConfigPage.codexDisableDesc: After disabling "{{name}}", Codex falls back to the built-in OpenAI provider. Sessions created under the disabled provider become hidden by Codex's provider filter. Enable the toggle below to migrate them to OpenAI so they remain visible.
clientConfigPage.codexSwitchRepairToggle: Also repair history sessions (migrate to new provider)

clientManage.title: Client Manager
clientManage.subtitle: Detect each AI client CLI's installed version, update to latest in one click, and diagnose multi-install conflicts for the same command
clientManage.conflictTitle: Multiple installations detected (update only affects the PATH-default one)

service.disabledHint: Enable the service to use these endpoints
service.modelsRefreshed: Models refreshed (re-fetched from the highest-tier account)
service.aliasHint: Routing by model name: prefix the model with a platform alias to target a provider explicitly, e.g. kr/claude-sonnet-4.5 (kr = local account pool); without a prefix it is matched by model name. See the model ids returned by /v1/models for available aliases.

combos.title: Route Combos
combos.desc: Chain several providers into an ordered fallback list, used as a single model name; on rate-limit/quota-exhaustion/error it auto-switches to the next step.
combos.namePlaceholder: Combo name (letters, digits, _.- ; no /, e.g. my-coding)
combos.empty: No combos yet — click "New combo" to create your first fallback chain.
combos.deleteDesc: Delete combo "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

clientKeys.title: Client Keys
clientKeys.emptyHint: Create a key to use it in the Authorization Bearer header
clientKeys.plaintextWarning: This key will not be shown again. Copy it now.

poolHealth.title: Proxy Pool
poolHealth.subtitle: Manage the reverse-proxy account pool: membership, selection priority and global selection policy; only pooled accounts are selected
poolHealth.emptyPoolWarning: The pool is empty: all proxied requests return 503. Add accounts to the pool first.
poolHealth.settingsDesc: These are global selection settings applied to the whole proxy pool; per-account priority is set inline in each row.
poolHealth.settingsStrategyHint: Sticky: reuse the same account within a session; round-robin: distribute evenly across accounts.
poolHealth.settingsAffinityHint: Within this window a session sticks to the same account; 0 disables stickiness.
poolHealth.settingsRateLimitCooldownHint: How long an account cools down after an upstream 429; accounts can override per-row (0=use global, -1=no cooldown).
poolHealth.settingsConcurrencyHint: Max simultaneous in-flight requests per account.
poolHealth.emptyHint: Click "Add" to pick accounts from Account Management
poolHealth.emptyNoCandidates: Add accounts of a pool-capable platform (e.g. Kiro) in Account Management first

addDialog.title: Add accounts to the pool
addDialog.desc: Select accounts to add (only pool-capable, not-yet-pooled accounts are shown)
addDialog.empty: No accounts available; add some in Account Management first

batchDialog.title: Batch set 429 rate-limit cooldown
batchDialog.desc: Set the post-429 cooldown for the selected accounts (overrides global)
batchDialog.cooldownHint: 0 = use global; -1 = no cooldown (immediate reuse); >0 = custom seconds

poolHealth.priorityTip: Selection weight: higher = larger traffic share (0 = default; pooled accounts only)
poolHealth.concurrencyTip: Max simultaneous in-flight requests per account (pooled only; default 4)

routingLog.title: Routing Logs
routingLog.subtitle: Persisted reverse-proxy request logs with multi-dimensional analysis (success rate / latency / fallback chain / tokens)
routingLog.clearDesc: This deletes all persisted request details and daily rollups. This cannot be undone.
routingLog.empty: No requests in this time range

sessionsView.subtitle: Conversation history from local AI tools
sessionsView.emptyDetail: Select a session to view details
sessionsView.emptyDetailSub: Click any session from the left panel
sessionsView.emptyListSub: Sessions will appear here after using this AI tool
sessionsView.confirmDeleteDesc: This action cannot be undone. Delete this session?
sessionsView.confirmDeleteBatchDesc: This will permanently delete {{count}} selected sessions.
sessionsView.repairExplain: Codex filters sessions by the current provider. When you switch providers, older sessions become invisible in Codex. Repairing merges sessions to the current provider, making them visible again.
sessionsView.repairCodexRunning: Codex is running. It will be safely stopped before repair and restarted afterward.
sessionsView.repairEncryptedNote: Encrypted sessions remain visible after switching providers, but continuing conversations may be limited by upstream restrictions.
sessionsView.repairDone: Repaired {{n}} sessions. Changes take effect after Codex restarts.
sessionsView.repairUnavailable: No repairable Codex session library detected
sessionsView.unsupportedSub: Session browsing for this client coming soon

faq.title: FAQ
items: How do new users authorize an account?
Error <System temporarily unavailable, please retry later.>
Why can't Bilibili-Open authorize a new account?

support_panel.title: Join the community
support_panel.desc: Scan to join our group for help and updates.

update.title: Software update
update.upToDate: You already have the latest version.
update.ready: The update is downloaded. Click "Install now" to restart and apply it.
update.readyManual: The installer is downloaded. Click "Open installer", then drag 号小管 into the Applications folder to install (manual install required on unsigned macOS).
update.errorHint: Couldn't complete the update. Please try again later.
